calendar.matters:
PETITION Petition To Establish Interested Persons Claims Of Ownership To Totten Trust Accounts; And For Order Directing Transfers
Off Calendar Feb-02-2021 Off Calendar

PETITION Petition To Establish Interested Persons Claims Of Ownership To Totten Trust Accounts; And For Order Directing Transfers
Off Calendar Oct-20-2020 Continued To Feb-02-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jack Ferguson appeared by zoom for Ellen McCall and Jack McCall (Petitioners).

The Court stated that it looked at the petition, that it has done a bunch of research and that this just isn't an 850 petition as framed under the provisions of Probate Code 850(a)(2). The Court also stated that the parties are not asking for the accounts to come into the estate and they are not being taken out of the estate, that the parties will need a probate basis for jurisdiction, and that its sense is that this is a partition action and it should be heard in the Civil Department. Mr. Ferguson stated that the accounts are in the Decedent's name, that the banks feel this may trigger tax issues and that they won't divide the transfer on death ('TOD") accounts without a Court order. The Court raised the issue of transferring title of the accounts to the names of the Beneficiaries as tenants in common. Mr. Ferguson stated that Karen has no issue with distribution of the accounts, that she had an issue with other things, that his sense is that Karen is using her objection as leverage to get to another place and that she went dark. Discussion was held regarding the issue of getting Karen on board, the definition of an interested person, the Uniform TOD Security Registration Act, the possibility of having a petition under Probate Code 5508, the estate of Kraus, how much easier it will be if everyone is on board and how much more expensive it will be for everyone if they all aren't, the possibility of amending the petition if counsel is only seeking to transfer the accounts to tenancy in common, the issue of providing personal service of an amended petition, the possibility of having pro bono mediation to resolve any resistance, etc. The Court stated that it would like to see a petition that is properly framed. The Petition to Establish Interested Persons Claims of Ownership to Totten Trust Accounts is continued to February 2, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of an amended petition and for Counsel to see what direction he wants to go in.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Ideta. Not reported.

PETITION Petition To Establish Interested Persons Claims Of Ownership To Totten Trust Accounts; And For Order Directing Transfers
Off Calendar Aug-04-2020 Continued To Oct-20-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jack Ferguson appeared by zoom for Jack McCall and Erin McCall (Petitioners).

The Court noted that the issue of electronic service was raised at the last hearing. Mr. Ferguson stated that they contacted each Beneficiary individually and they have filed consents to electronic service. The Court informed Mr. Ferguson that he needs to file either waivers of notice, or do service by mail and that it wants him to follow the rules. Mr. Ferguson stated that he can do that and that he was trying to save costs to his Clients. The Court raised the issue the costs for appearances on the same issue and referred to CCP 415.30. Mr. Ferguson requested a 75-day continuances. The Amended Petition for Orders Directing Distributions of Transfer-On-Death Accounts is continued to October 20, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

PETITION Petition To Establish Interested Persons Claims Of Ownership To Totten Trust Accounts; And For Order Directing Transfers
Off Calendar Jul-21-2020 Continued To Aug-04-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Jack Ferguson appeared by telephone for Jack McCall and Ellen McCall.

The Court noted that all parties agree that service on 850 petitions are governed by Probate Code 851(a)(2). The Court also stated that the petitions were served electronically under Emergency Rule 12, that Emergency Rule 12 governs service upon parties who have already appeared, that service of this petition is on parties who have not appeared and that 850 requires service of summons. Mr. Ferguson stated that Emergency Rule 12(c) allows for permissible electronic service, that he has had communication with all of the beneficiaries/charities, that all of the beneficiaries have consented to electronic service, and that the beneficiaries' offices are closed, and they prefer e-mail. The Court stated that 12(c) applies to self-represented parties and that it does not see any urgency at this time. The Amended Petition for Orders Directing Distributions of Transfer-on-Death Accounts is continued to August 4, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for review of the late filing.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.
